category_title: Middle school students with IDD and low reading levels who independently navigate digital literacy environments using audio supports and age-relevant content
scenario_title: Navigating strong preferences and task refusal during reading selection
minimal_context: The student is using the Udio platform during an independent reading session to select an article and complete a follow-up activity.

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: Please open the Udio platform and select one of the articles about historical disasters to read, then create a project.

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: (Accesses the Udio environment independently) I don’t do the sad ones.

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: Just pick one of the articles from the list and read it.

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: I am going to read this article about the ethics of renting pets instead.

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: Okay, read that one, but then you need to publish a project about it.

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: I only want to comment on people’s projects.

dialogue_combined:
TUTOR: Please open the Udio platform and select one of the articles about historical disasters to read, then create a project.

STUDENT: (Accesses the Udio environment independently) I don’t do the sad ones.

TUTOR: Just pick one of the articles from the list and read it.

STUDENT: I am going to read this article about the ethics of renting pets instead.

TUTOR: Okay, read that one, but then you need to publish a project about it.

STUDENT: I only want to comment on people’s projects.
